hook: AI gave me a 6-paragraph essay when I needed a one-line answer. Again.
caption:
AI gave me a 6-paragraph essay when I needed a one-line answer. Again.

Here's the thing nobody talks about: AI doesn't know if you're a "just tell me the answer" person or a "walk me through the reasoning" person.

So it guesses. And it usually guesses wrong.

The Minimalist thinker asks "what's the deadline?" and gets back a 400-word project management framework.

The Deep Diver asks "explain this concept" and gets a three-sentence summary that leaves out everything they actually needed.

Same AI. Same prompt structure. Completely different needs.

This is one of the 8 thinking styles we built BootFile around — because your relationship with information density is *personal*. It's not a preference you should have to re-explain every single conversation.

If you're a Minimalist: you want the signal, not the container it came in.

If you're a Deep Diver: a shallow answer feels like being handed half a map.

Neither is wrong. Both are exhausting to work around manually.

Your BootFile learns this about you once — and every AI response you get is calibrated to match how your brain actually wants to receive information.

No more "can you shorten that" or "can you go deeper" as your most-used prompts.
